summary: A handwritten 1970 letter to James L. Tuck recalls green light sightings observed at Los Alamos between 1948 and 1951, typically between 9 and 11 pm over the Jemez Mountains, along with one afternoon sighting of five objects flying in formation from southeast to northwest; the writer notes these were reported to the Protective Force at the time and suggests checking Protective Force logs for exact dates. A separate related letter from Tuck to a U.S. Army Engineering School requests information on a 'recipe' used for simulated atomic bomb demonstrations, referencing atmospheric vortices described in Edward U. Condon's UFO study.
